name: Chole Bhature
tagline: Spiced chickpea curry with cloud-light fried bread
shortTagline: spiced chickpea curry · fried bread · 60 min

hero.alt: Chole bhature on a cream ceramic plate with dark chole, puffed bhature, onion, and lemon
hero.caption: chole bhature — dark chole, puffed bhatura

healthFacts: Chickpeas deliver 15g fiber and 12g protein per cup — one of the most filling legumes.
Amchur (dried mango powder) adds vitamin C, which boosts iron absorption from chickpeas.
Pomegranate seeds in chole are a traditional garnish rich in antioxidant punicalagins.
The tangy, acidic cooking environment (amchur + tomatoes) helps reduce lectin content.
Bhature uses yogurt — the lactic acid makes the bread tender and partially fermented.
Tea bag trick: cooking chole with a black tea bag gives deeper color and mild tannins.

steps.title: Soak the chickpeas
steps.detail: Wash and soak dried chickpeas in 4 cups of water for 8 hours or overnight. They'll nearly double. Drain and rinse. If using canned, rinse well and skip to step 3.
steps.tip: Soaking reduces oligosaccharides that cause bloating. Worth the overnight wait.

media.alt: Soaked kabuli chana in a steel bowl with clear water
media.caption: Overnight-soaked chickpeas

steps.title: Make the bhature dough
steps.detail: Mix flour, baking powder, and salt. Add yogurt and oil. Knead into a soft, pliable dough, adding water tablespoon by tablespoon. Cover with a damp cloth and rest for 30 minutes — this is when the gluten relaxes for puffing.
steps.tip: Softer dough = better puff. The dough should feel like soft earlobe texture.

media.alt: Soft bhature dough resting under a damp cloth in a steel bowl
media.caption: Soft rested dough

steps.title: Pressure cook the chole
steps.detail: Add soaked chickpeas to a pressure cooker with fresh water (just covering), salt, and a tea bag. Cook for 5–6 whistles until completely soft but holding shape. Discard the tea bag. Reserve the cooking water.
steps.tip: The black tea bag gives chole that characteristic deep brownish color. It's not optional.

media.alt: Cooked chickpeas in a pressure cooker with tea-stained cooking water
media.caption: Tea-stained cooked chole

steps.title: Build the masala
steps.detail: Heat oil in a kadhai. Add finely chopped onions and cook 8–10 minutes until deep golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook 2 minutes. Add pureed tomatoes, chole masala, Kashmiri chili, and amchur. Cook until oil separates — about 8 minutes.
steps.tip: The masala must be cooked down until it's dark, thick, and jammy. Don't cut this short.

media.alt: Dark onion-tomato chole masala in a black kadhai with oil separating
media.caption: Jammy masala base

steps.title: Simmer the chole
steps.detail: Add cooked chickpeas to the masala with 1 cup of the reserved cooking water. Mix, mash a few chickpeas against the side of the pan for body. Simmer uncovered 15–20 minutes until the gravy is thick and dark.
steps.tip: Good chole is dry-ish and dark. If it looks watery and pale, keep simmering.

media.alt: Chickpeas simmering in thick dark masala gravy in a black kadhai
media.caption: Thick simmered chole

steps.title: Fry the bhature
steps.detail: Roll bhature dough into balls. Flatten each into an oval, 5–6mm thick. Heat oil to 180°C. Slide in one bhatura — press gently with a slotted spoon, it will puff. Flip once, fry 30 seconds more. Drain and serve immediately with hot chole.
steps.tip: Oil must be hot enough — test with a small dough piece; it should rise immediately.

media.alt: A puffed bhatura frying in hot oil under a slotted spoon
media.caption: Puffed bhatura fry
